description: Consumer repo governance drift — submodule staleness, empty state directories, and stale CLAUDE.md references cluster together. When one repo drifts, all repos drift, because Demerzel's governance cadence affects all consumers equally. The pattern is: Demerzel evolves rapidly (142-144 commits) while consumers stand still, creating a widening gap between governance intent and governance reality.
root_cause: Demerzel development velocity outpaces consumer repo integration cadence. No automated mechanism to propagate governance updates. Submodule pinning creates intentional lag but with no refresh trigger, lag becomes drift.
recommended_action: Establish governance sync cadence — weekly submodule updates at minimum. Consider CI/CD automation to detect submodule staleness >7 days. Create Galactic Protocol 'sync_request' message type.
constitutional_articles: Article 8 (Observability) — drift was invisible until manual recon
Article 9 (Bounded Autonomy) — consumer agents operating outside current governance bounds
Asimov Article 0 (Zeroth Law) — ungoverned agents represent systemic risk
